content.Text: Now update the call in `signals.ts` to pass liquidity data:

ToolUse.raw_input: {"replace_all":false,"file_path":"/Users/lucfaucheux/.openclaw/workspace/kalshi-weather/src/signals.ts","old_string":"  logPaperTrade(city, dateStr, contract.market.ticker, contract.side, contract.price, consensusHighF, impliedTempF, gapF, spread, confidence, ensemblePWin, ensembleStdDev, ensembleHighs.length);","new_string":"  logPaperTrade(city, dateStr, contract.market.ticker, contract.side, contract.price, consensusHighF, impliedTempF, gapF, spread, confidence, ensemblePWin, ensembleStdDev, ensembleHighs.length, liqCheck.hasLiquidity, liqCheck.spread);"}

input.old_string: logPaperTrade(city, dateStr, contract.market.ticker, contract.side, contract.price, consensusHighF, impliedTempF, gapF, spread, confidence, ensemblePWin, ensembleStdDev, ensembleHighs.length);
input.new_string: logPaperTrade(city, dateStr, contract.market.ticker, contract.side, contract.price, consensusHighF, impliedTempF, gapF, spread, confidence, ensemblePWin, ensembleStdDev, ensembleHighs.length, liqCheck.hasLiquidity, liqCheck.spread);

content.Text:
No signals triggered this run (all cities were filtered out by confidence/spread/gap checks before reaching the liquidity check). That's expected — the liquidity check sits after all the existing filters, so it only fires when there's actually a tradeable signal.

Let me verify the code compiles cleanly with a type check:

Clean — no TypeScript errors.

Here's a quick test to confirm the orderbook endpoint works at all:

ToolUse.name: curl -s "https://api.elections.kalshi.com/trade-api/v2/markets/KXHIGHNY-26APR22-B57.5/orderbook" 2>&1 | python3 -m json.tool 2>/dev/null | head -30
ToolUse.raw_input: {"command":"curl -s \"https://api.elections.kalshi.com/trade-api/v2/markets/KXHIGHNY-26APR22-B57.5/orderbook\" 2>&1 | python3 -m json.tool 2>/dev/null | head -30","description":"Test orderbook API endpoint","timeout":15000}

content.Text: The orderbook endpoint works unauthenticated. However, the response format uses `orderbook_fp` with `no_dollars`/`yes_dollars` (dollar-based) rather than `orderbook` with `yes_bids`/`yes_asks` (cents-based). Let me check the full structure:
